_meta.source_primary: Andrieu, Hillier & Birch (2006) Annals of Botany 98:1005-1016
_meta.fitting_method: Hillier, Makowski & Andrieu (2005) Annals of Botany 96:137-148
_meta.cultivar_source: Dea (temperate French dent), Grignon, normal density 9.5 plants m^-2
_meta.precision_note: Vision-read approximations of Figs 6 (R1), 7 (lag_exp), 9 (R2), 10 (D_lin). +/-15% global. For publication-grade precision use Vidal 2021 SupData3 (SupData3-fit_3phase_models_s2.xlsx, PMC7877697) for M40/M52 inbred-derived alternates, or request original Hillier-et-al. ML fits.
_meta.unit_caveat_R2_published: R2_cm_per_Cd_published values from artifact (~2-6 cm/Cd) are systematically ~10x the kinematically realistic LER for maize (Tardieu/Reymond, Birch report 0.2-1.0 cm/Cd). Likely a unit-label mismatch in the figure-read source (mm/Cd printed as cm/Cd) or a figure-axis misread. Carried verbatim for traceability; superseded by R2_cm_per_Cd_rescaled which is anchored on MF3D L_fin via C^1 continuity and lands in the kinematic literature range.
_meta.Tb_harmonisation_note: Tb=9.8 used for both leaf and stem clocks per ADR D3. Andrieu 2006 publishes Tb=9.7; difference within fitting CI. Single Tb avoids artificial offsets between leaf and stem submodels.
_meta.birth_gate_separation: T0_Cd here is the Andrieu phase-E origin on the Tb=9.8 axis (M7), NOT the leaf birth gate. The birth gate is ldelay + ldelayAxis=TT on LeafRandomParameter (post-Lock #8, CPlantBox ff30912e, S0.9). Bake script must satisfy T0_Cd >= ldelay per leaf subType: a leaf cannot enter Andrieu phase E before it is born. Validation gate active once XML is baked with axis="TT" ldelay values.
_meta.mf3d_anchor_note: R2_cm_per_Cd_rescaled is computed under C^1 continuity (Andrieu 2006 eq. C.3) with R1 and D_lin held at published figure-read values, anchoring L_fin on MF3D lmax_n at the corresponding position (per ADR D2). Sanity flag R2_rescale_flag fires when the rescaled-to-published ratio is outside [0.7, 1.3] (per ADR C5). Formula: L1 = L_fin_MF3D / (1 + R1 * D_lin); R2_rescaled = R1 * L1.
_meta.extrapolation_note: Ranks 16-17 linearly extrapolated from rank 13-15 slope per ADR C4: R1 slope = -0.005/rank; R2_published slope = -1.0/rank; lag_exp constant plateau at 150 Cd; D_lin slope = -20/rank with floor 15 Cd to avoid degeneracy. Mirrors stem-side phase_III_per_rank.json extrapolation pattern. lag_exp_Cd_implied diverges sharply (>250 Cd) for these ranks because MF3D lmax at top positions (44-50 cm) is much larger than Dea flag-leaf lengths.
_meta.position_mapping_note: Per ADR C4: positions 0-1 of our 16-leaf XML are FA-S3b stubs (project_16leaf_extension memory) and represent Dea juvenile ranks 1-3 (all gated). Positions 2-15 map to Dea ranks 4-17 1:1 (rank n -> position n-2). Cultivar offset between MF3D peak (position 6) and Dea peak (rank 11) is absorbed by the per-rank R2 rescaling.
_meta.leaf_subtype_mapping_note: In maize_calibrated.xml, <leaf subType="N"/> uses N = Dea rank directly (subType 2 = stub for rank 2, ..., subType 17 = rank 17). The bake script writes per-rank arrays into the matching subType; gated ranks (1-3) get zero-valued kinetic fields.

cultivar_mismatch_summary.note: All 14 fitted ranks flagged. Two compounding causes: (1) the published R2 figure-reads are systematically ~10x kinematic literature values (see unit_caveat_R2_published); (2) MF3D peak at position 6 = Dea rank 8 vs Andrieu Dea peak LER at rank 11. Per-rank rescaling absorbs both. The flag count is informative (large divergence) but not a model defect: the rescaled values land 0.47-0.91 cm/Cd, in the kinematic literature range. ADR C5 documents this is the expected outcome when figure-read source and MF3D anchor disagree.
